notes.anchor: thank you for compelling me to live
notes.note: A recurring note of the exile letters: Cicero believed Atticus had talked him out of suicide, and oscillates between gratitude and bitter regret at being kept alive. The reproach 'I am bitterly sorry I am alive' echoes through 3.4 and 3.5.
Vibo (Vibo Valentia) was a town on the coast of Bruttium in the far south-west of Italy, where Cicero had turned aside on his flight. He summons Atticus there to plan the sea-crossing into exile.
